auditor: Chartered Professional Accountants, Licensed Public Accountants
economic_notes: First Nation owns 100% of IIFN39 Holdings LP which carries on various economic development and commercial enterprises including Shoal Lake Lodge LP, Ambe Arts and Crafts LP, Amik II Barge LP. Also operates Shoal Lake Landing store and Mr. Bubbles Laundromat. Ongoing land claims negotiations with Government of Canada and Province of Ontario regarding farming lands, Garden Islands and flooding claims.
notes: Audit is qualified on two bases: (1) First Nation has not completed assessment of investments in business enterprises (IIFN39 Holdings LP and IIFN39 Holdings Inc.) to determine if they should be fully consolidated rather than accounted for under modified equity method; (2) Tangible capital asset records are incomplete as only assets acquired since 1996 are recorded and useful lives/conditions have not been reviewed. Financial statements are for year ended March 31, 2021 but were signed January 22, 2024 - a significant delay. Long-term debt figure of 4,767,812 includes both long-term debt of 3,460,777 and capital lease obligations of 1,307,035. Annual debt service reflects only long-term debt principal repayment per cash flow statement; capital lease repayments were 339,186. No schedule of remuneration was included in the provided text. Education expenses in segmented disclosure show 3,813,189 for the segment but 3,806,624 in the statement of operations due to segmented allocation differences; statement of operations figure used.
